2603.16969.title: DeepStage: Learning Autonomous Defense Policies Against Multi-Stage APT Campaigns
2603.16969.paper_summary: This paper presents DeepStage, a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework for adaptive, stage-aware defense against Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs). The enterprise environment is modeled as a partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP), where host provenance and network telemetry are fused into unified provenance graphs. Building on our prior work, StageFinder, a graph neural encoder and an LSTM-based stage estimator infer probabilistic attacker stages aligned with the MITRE ATT&CK framework. These stage beliefs, combined with graph embeddings, guide a hierarchical Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) agent that selects defense actions across monitoring, access control, containment, and remediation. Evaluated in a realistic enterprise testbed using CALDERA-driven APT playbooks, DeepStage achieves a stage-weighted F1-score of 0.89, outperforming a risk-aware DRL baseline by 21.9%. The results demonstrate effective stage-aware and cost-efficient autonomous cyber defense.
2603.16969.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文介紹了DeepStage，一個用於對抗高級持續威脅（APTs）的自適應階段感知防禦的深度強化學習（DRL）框架。企業環境被建模為一個部分可觀察的馬可夫決策過程（POMDP），其中主機來源和網絡遙測被融合成統一的來源圖。基於我們之前的工作，StageFinder，一個圖神經編碼器和基於LSTM的階段估計器推斷與MITRE ATT&CK框架對齊的概率攻擊者階段。這些階段信念，結合圖嵌入，指導一個分層的近端策略優化（PPO）代理，該代理選擇監控、訪問控制、遏制和補救等防禦行動。在使用CALDERA驅動的APT行動手冊的現實企業測試平台中進行評估，DeepStage達到了0.89的階段加權F1分數，超越了風險感知的DRL基準21.9%。結果顯示了有效的階段感知和成本效益的自主網絡防禦。
2603.16969.author: Trung V. Phan et.al.
2603.16969.authors: Trung V. Phan, Tri Gia Nguyen, Thomas Bauschert
